title: Flight Registration
description: The system shall allow air traffic controllers to register incoming and outgoing flights with fields: flight number, airline, origin/destination, scheduled time, and type (arrival/departure).
rationale: This requirement is necessary to ensure all flight operations are recorded and managed within the system.
business_value: Enhanced operational efficiency and safety through accurate and timely flight information management.

source.description: As an air traffic controller, I need to register incoming and outgoing flights so that all flight operations are recorded and managed within the system.

acceptance_criteria: The flight is recorded in the system
They can edit or cancel the flight
They can view the list of all flights
preconditions: The air traffic controller is on the flight management dashboard
postconditions: The new flight is added to the system database
Existing flight data is updated if edited
Flight registration history is maintained for future reference
functional_constraints: All required fields (flight number, airline, origin/destination, scheduled time, type) must be filled out for a successful registration
Edit or cancel operations should only affect the specific flight in question without altering other records
security_requirements: Data transmission between the controller and system should be encrypted to prevent interception of sensitive information
Access to flight registration features should be restricted based on user roles (e.g., only authorized personnel can edit or cancel flights)

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should handle up to 100 concurrent flight registrations without significant delay
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time to ensure uninterrupted operation
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to support at least 5000 registered flights without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages for incomplete or invalid inputs should be clear and actionable, and the interface should be intuitive for air traffic controllers

edge_cases: What happens if a flight number is already registered?
How should the system handle time zone differences between flights and the controller's location?
What are the consequences of attempting to edit or cancel a non-existent flight?

interfaces.name: Flight Registration API
interfaces.description: The system should provide an API to register, edit, and cancel flights.

interfaces.name: Flight Data Store
interfaces.description: The system should interact with a database to store flight registration data, including updates and deletions.

interfaces.name: Flight Management Dashboard
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the user interface where air traffic controllers can perform flight registration operations.

test_cases.expected_result: A new flight record is created with the provided details and marked as an arrival.

test_cases.description: Edit an existing departure flight's scheduled time.
test_cases.expected_result: The specified departure flight's scheduled time is updated in the database.
The registered incoming flight record is marked as cancelled and removed from active listings.

test_cases.description: Attempt to register a flight with incomplete details.
test_cases.expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating missing required fields.
